scope.summary: Go select receive statements now preserve comma-ok channel status as the existing channel-receive-status protocol obligation.

capability_alignment.capability: channel receive value/status provenance inside select communication cases
not_a_feature_list: No new public semantic-pack API was added.
No new kernel enum or Go selector-specific admission path was added.
The change reuses SourceProtocolKind::ChannelReceive and channel-receive-status-contract.

corpus_signal.note: This lexical count prices select communication cases with comma-ok receives; it is source-prevalence evidence, not exact semantic proof.

source_surfaces: func status(ch chan int) bool { _, ok := <-ch; return ok }
func selectStatus(ch chan int) bool { select { case _, ok := <-ch: return ok; default: return false } }

current_crates_gate.note: The unit delta comes from the added Rust test fixtures; the Go select status recovery does not open exact admission.

assertions: A Go select communication case with _, ok := <-ch preserves a ChannelReceive status protocol boundary.
The select parent and select case protocol boundaries remain present.
The select subtree reports select readiness/case obligations and nested channel-receive-status/value obligations.
Go goroutine, defer, send, receive, comma-ok receive, and select receive protocol exclusions are attributed under structured obligations.
The select comma-ok unit includes channel-receive-status-contract in missing_evidence while select readiness remains the primary rollup.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between select receive status and synchronous boolean values
closed_surfaces.reason: Close/zero-value behavior, select readiness, and case-selection contracts remain missing.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between channel receive value and ordinary value reads
closed_surfaces.reason: Blocking, synchronization, and close semantics remain protocol obligations.

closed_surfaces.surface: Exact convergence between goroutine/defer calls and direct calls
closed_surfaces.reason: Scheduling, callback effect, and defer ordering contracts remain missing.

next_followups: Price and report channel close/zero-value behavior separately before considering exact channel-status recovery.
Keep select readiness and case-selection primary over nested send/receive operations until complete select semantics are proven.
Consider Go goroutine/defer callback identity/effect proof as a separate slice.
